repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Allows using `buf` option for `vim.keymap.set()` and `vim.keymap.del()`, following the `:h dev-name-common` conventions.

The `buffer` option remains functional but is now undocumented. Providing both will raise an error. Since providing `buf` was disallowed before, there is no code that will break due to using `buffer` alongside `buf`.

Replaces all usages of `buffer` in docs, tests, and runtime files. The changes are split into separate commits for the convenience. 

Incremental step towards #35330.
Supersedes #36734.
Fix #35316.

comment_data.body: Instead of doing this switch I would directly deprecate `buffer` in `opts` and if set use that value to set `buf`. We do something similar with `opts.float` (deprecated) to set `opts.on_jump` [here](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/875958c9a1d1cd7454e66376676995fc2655a9d4/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua#L1238-L1252).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -83,14 +83,22 @@ function keymap.set(modes, lhs, rhs, opts)
     rhs = ''
   end
 
-  if opts.buffer then
-    local bufnr = opts.buffer == true and 0 or opts.buffer --[[@as integer]]
-    opts.buffer = nil ---@type integer?
+  --- @cast opts +{buffer:integer|boolean}

@@ -83,14 +83,22 @@ function keymap.set(modes, lhs, rhs, opts)
     rhs = ''
   end
 
-  if opts.buffer then
-    local bufnr = opts.buffer == true and 0 or opts.buffer --[[@as integer]]
-    opts.buffer = nil ---@type integer?
+  local buf = opts.buf
+  opts.buf = nil
+  --- @cast opts +{buffer?:integer|boolean}
+  if opts.buffer ~= nil then
+    -- TODO(skewb1k): soft-deprecate `buffer` option in 0.13, remove in 0.15.
+    assert(buf == nil, "Conflict: 'buf' not allowed with 'buffer'")

comment_data.body:
I just saw this: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/36734#issuecomment-3590002758

I personally has no opinion on this, but I understand why one might want to slow down the deprecation process in that case. My only concern is that this approach not follows the strategy outlined in MAINTAIN.md. Something being undocumented but not marked with `vim.deprecate` doesn't feel right.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -83,14 +83,22 @@ function keymap.set(modes, lhs, rhs, opts)
     rhs = ''
   end
 
-  if opts.buffer then
-    local bufnr = opts.buffer == true and 0 or opts.buffer --[[@as integer]]
-    opts.buffer = nil ---@type integer?
+  local buf = opts.buf
+  opts.buf = nil
+  --- @cast opts +{buffer?:integer|boolean}
+  if opts.buffer ~= nil then
+    -- TODO(skewb1k): soft-deprecate `buffer` option in 0.13, remove in 0.15.
+    assert(buf == nil, "Conflict: 'buf' not allowed with 'buffer'")

@@ -83,14 +83,22 @@ function keymap.set(modes, lhs, rhs, opts)
     rhs = ''
   end
 
-  if opts.buffer then
-    local bufnr = opts.buffer == true and 0 or opts.buffer --[[@as integer]]
-    opts.buffer = nil ---@type integer?
+  local buf = opts.buf

+  --- @cast opts +{buffer?:integer|boolean}
+  if opts.buffer ~= nil then
+    -- TODO(skewb1k): soft-deprecate `buffer` option in 0.13, remove in 0.15.
+    assert(buf == nil, "Conflict: 'buf' not allowed with 'buffer'")

comment_data.body: This will trigger a massive round of deprecation warnings not only in user configs but also in plugins, requiring adding tedious compat shims for nothing but a rename. We _need_ a softer deprecation process for such pure naming changes (since there's no benefit for downstream from this such as bugfixes, performance, or saner behavior).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -83,14 +83,22 @@ function keymap.set(modes, lhs, rhs, opts)
     rhs = ''
   end
 
-  if opts.buffer then
-    local bufnr = opts.buffer == true and 0 or opts.buffer --[[@as integer]]
-    opts.buffer = nil ---@type integer?
+  local buf = opts.buf
+  opts.buf = nil
+  --- @cast opts +{buffer?:integer|boolean}
+  if opts.buffer ~= nil then
+    -- TODO(skewb1k): soft-deprecate `buffer` option in 0.13, remove in 0.15.
+    assert(buf == nil, "Conflict: 'buf' not allowed with 'buffer'")

comment_data.body: I noticed that in many other places, for example LSP filters, the `true` value is not allowed and just having `integer?` covers all the cases. How much do we actually need to support boolean here? Maybe we can drop it to simplify API if we introducing new option anyway?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -430,12 +430,12 @@ want to defer the loading to the time when the mapping is executed (as for
 The fourth, optional, argument is a table with keys that modify the behavior
 of the mapping such as those from |:map-arguments|. The following are the most
 useful options:
-• `buffer`: If given, only set the mapping for the buffer with the specified
+• `buf`: If given, only set the mapping for the buffer with the specified
   number; `0` or `true` means the current buffer. >lua

comment_data.body:
> Maybe we can drop it to deduplicate API surface if we introducing new option anyway?

SGTM. idk where that "boolean" variant came from. We should drop that from the lua-guide.txt too.

Edit: oh, I wonder if the boolean is because we may *return* `buf=false` to indicate "not buffer-local".
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -430,12 +430,12 @@ want to defer the loading to the time when the mapping is executed (as for
 The fourth, optional, argument is a table with keys that modify the behavior
 of the mapping such as those from |:map-arguments|. The following are the most
 useful options:
-• `buffer`: If given, only set the mapping for the buffer with the specified
+• `buf`: If given, only set the mapping for the buffer with the specified
   number; `0` or `true` means the current buffer. >lua

comment_data.body:
> Edit: oh, I wonder if the boolean is because we may _return_ `buf=false` to indicate "not buffer-local".

Hmm, so it is for compatibility with some other API? I couldn't find any function that returns boolean for a buffer. But either if there is one, `vim.keymap` [treats](https://github.com/skewb1k/neovim/blob/bef68ba266038b94a532ce76e315dd71089939f3/runtime/lua/vim/keymap.lua#L85) `false` as `nil` and `true` as `0`, so this conversion can be easily done at the call site.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -430,12 +430,12 @@ want to defer the loading to the time when the mapping is executed (as for
 The fourth, optional, argument is a table with keys that modify the behavior
 of the mapping such as those from |:map-arguments|. The following are the most
 useful options:
-• `buffer`: If given, only set the mapping for the buffer with the specified
+• `buf`: If given, only set the mapping for the buffer with the specified
   number; `0` or `true` means the current buffer. >lua

comment_data.body: Yes, this was originally done this way to mirror `:map <buffer>` as a boolean flag; being able to target non-current buffers came as an addition. Not sure how widely used this is in practice, so _this_ could be aggressively deprecated since it's easy to adjust to.

@@ -430,12 +430,12 @@ want to defer the loading to the time when the mapping is executed (as for
 The fourth, optional, argument is a table with keys that modify the behavior

+• `buf`: If given, only set the mapping for the buffer with the specified
   number; `0` or `true` means the current buffer. >lua

 The fourth, optional, argument is a table with keys that modify the behavior
 of the mapping such as those from |:map-arguments|. The following are the most

   number; `0` or `true` means the current buffer. >lua

comment_regular_data.body:
> Also `bufnr` in the (new!) lsp `enable` functions.

Based on `dev-naming` and previous discussion (https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/35316#issuecomment-3181034560, https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/36459#issuecomment-3519853295) it seems like `buf` is preferable? Maybe it should be used in a new yet-unreleased places like `document_color.enable()` too... What is the solution?

Honestly, I'd prefer `bufnr` as I think that's the most common across the different current APIs. (`autocmd` API uses `buffer`, but that's slightly different since it comes from `:au`.)

But it should be consistent across the Lua API, so the inlay hints and document color functions should match what ~is~ was decided here. (No deprecation/compatibility needed for the functions introduced in the 0.12 dev cycle, but it costs little to add anyway to avoid needless consistency paper cuts.)
I suggest `buf` to avoid new discussion and mixed messages in the developer docs.

labels.description: Nvim defaults for options, colorscheme/theme, autocmds/events, etc.

title: feat(lua): replace `buffer` with `buf` in vim.keymap.set/del
